recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMZN from 12.35% to 10% given only neutral +0.01 sentiment and better opportunities in higher-conviction names like LLY.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN headlines are mixed with AI spend focus but no standout positive catalyst today; position is the largest in portfolio and trimming creates dry powder for new LLY entry without exiting the name; respects recent decision history by not repeating any prior AMZN trades.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 1% position in Deere on positive sentiment (+0.32) and the appointment of a new CFO, which could boost investor confidence.
recent_trades.reasoning: Deere has underperformed recently (-1.43% over 30 days), but the positive news about the new CFO and a cautiously optimistic outlook may catalyze a turnaround. A small starter position allows us to participate while managing risk.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD from 4.2% to 3.2% to take profit on a 17.7% gain and reduce exposure to negative sentiment (VADER -0.19).
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD has rallied strongly and now faces negative news sentiment. Trimming 1% of portfolio value locks in gains while leaving a meaningful core position intact. The proceeds will fund a new buy in Deere.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4% position in JPMorgan Chase to capitalize on a technical breakout setup and highly positive sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: JPMorgan Chase is consolidating constructively near its 30-day highs and flashing a strong technical breakout setup. Combined with strong sentiment (+0.36), this justifies initiating a solid core position.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Caterpillar to a 5% allocation to ride strong relative momentum and a new Street-high price target.
recent_trades.reasoning: Caterpillar is seeing highly positive sentiment (+0.43) driven by top analyst calls and a new Street-high price target. Increasing the position to 5% positions the portfolio to benefit from this industrial strength.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing UnitedHealth to 2.5% following reports of a top insider trimming their stake after the recent earnings rally.
recent_trades.reasoning: While UNH has rallied recently, news of top insider selling warrants caution. Trimming the position locks in some of the +4.49% unrealized gains and reallocates capital to setups with stronger near-term upside catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Qualcomm to 4% to cut exposure to a technical laggard languishing near its 30-day lows and free up capital.
recent_trades.reasoning: Qualcomm has been underperforming with a -2.96% unrealized loss and is trading near the bottom of its recent range. Trimming this position frees up necessary liquidity to deploy into higher-conviction momentum and breakout plays.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Apple to reach a 7% weight, riding its strong post-earnings breakout and positive momentum lifting the broader tech market.
recent_trades.reasoning: Apple's narrative has rapidly shifted positive following earnings, with headlines indicating a strong bid from tech bulls. Adding to our position captures this upward momentum while keeping the portfolio securely aligned with the current market leaders.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Alphabet to a 10% core position as it continues to dominate the AI narrative with peer-leading sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Alphabet is exhibiting tremendous post-earnings relative strength and the news sentiment is overwhelmingly positive (+0.39). Increasing the weight to 10% reflects high conviction in its ongoing breakout and dominant market position.

recent_trades.summary: Reducing Intel to a 3% weight to harvest profits and free up capital for stronger post-earnings momentum plays.
recent_trades.reasoning: Intel is down nearly 0.8% today and lacks the fundamental catalysts needed for a sustained breakout at this juncture. Trimming the position monetizes a ~4.7% gain and optimizes our capital allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD to a 3% weight to lock in gains and reduce exposure amid negative sentiment and rotation into mega-cap leaders.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD is showing slightly negative sentiment and is struggling to keep pace with broader tech momentum today. Halving the position secures our unrealized profit while freeing up necessary capital to deploy into stronger opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Raising GOOGL to 7% on strong +0.39 sentiment and AI leadership in a record Nasdaq session.
recent_trades.reasoning: GOOGL benefits from Nasdaq record headlines and questions on its AI positioning post-earnings; current 5.4% weight offers room to add. This reallocation captures tech momentum while keeping overall portfolio balanced.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing PFE to 8% on very positive +0.52 sentiment and quality pharma tailwinds.
recent_trades.reasoning: PFE tops sentiment rankings with headlines on passive income, patient access, and analyst coverage; technicals remain stable near recent highs. Modest add from ~5% deploys trimmed capital into a defensive growth name with strong conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting TSLA entirely due to negative sentiment and better opportunities elsewhere.
recent_trades.reasoning: TSLA carries -0.18 sentiment with headlines centered on compensation rather than operational strength; despite a 1.2% intraday gain the risk/reward lags high-sentiment holdings. Full exit aligns with recent reallocation toward AI/pharma leaders.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMD to 2% on negative sentiment and today's 1.1% decline.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMD's -0.19 sentiment reflects mixed AI headlines versus stronger peers; technicals show a red candle below recent highs. Reducing from ~5% frees capital for higher-conviction names without violating position limits.

recent_trades.summary: Buying Netflix on positive sentiment from new product launches and mixed shareholder returns, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NFLX sentiment is positive (+0.23) with news of 'Clips' vertical video feed. The stock is trading near its 30-day high with steady volume. A 2% position allows participation in streaming growth while managing risk.

recent_trades.summary: Adding Cisco Systems on its recent earnings beat and positive market outperformance, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CSCO has strong positive sentiment (+0.26) and recently beat the market. The stock is near its 30-day high, supported by solid fundamentals and networking demand. Initiating a 2% position fits portfolio balance.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Adobe to free up capital for higher-conviction plays in networking and streaming, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ADBE is a small position (1.99%) with modest gains and neutral sentiment. Reallocating to CSCO and NFLX, which have stronger recent earnings and positive news flow, should improve risk/reward.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating Caterpillar on positive analyst call momentum and steady trading just above the lower end of its recent range, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: CAT has one of the strongest sentiment readings in this snapshot, supported by fresh bullish analyst commentary and a new street-high price target. Price is slightly soft intraday, which offers a reasonable entry into an industrial name with improving narrative support, and a 2.5% starter size keeps risk controlled.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Broadcom on persistent AI-capex support and constructive price action near the top of its recent range, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO continues to benefit from bullish data-center and AI infrastructure headlines, and the stock is holding close to its 30-day high rather than breaking down after a strong run. The current 2.1% weight is modest for a high-conviction semiconductor winner, so increasing it to 4.0% improves exposure without approaching concentration limits.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the small EOG position because the news catalyst is weak and the position is too small to matter compared with stronger ideas elsewhere, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: EOG has neutral news flow, sits below its 30-day high, and lacks the clearer company-specific support seen in COP or the payment and pharma names. Since the position is only about 1% of portfolio value, consolidating it into stronger holdings should improve portfolio efficiency without materially increasing concentration.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Qualcomm as it remains one of the weaker tech holdings with negative sentiment and price action stuck near its 30-day low, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM is down versus cost basis, carries mildly negative headline sentiment, and is trading close to the bottom of its 30-day range while peers tied to AI infrastructure are acting better. This is a relative-strength trim rather than a full exit, reducing exposure from 4.8% to 3.0% and freeing capital for higher-conviction names.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Intel from 3.1% to 2.0% to take profit on a 38% gain, freeing cash for tactical deployment.
recent_trades.reasoning: INTC has gained over 38% since entry, now one of the largest gainers in the portfolio. With no new near-term catalysts for further upside, reducing exposure locks in gains and reduces single-stock risk. The proceeds will be held as cash for potential re-entry on pullbacks or new opportunities.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Alphabet allocation to 7.5% to ride its continued post-earnings surge and undisputed relative strength.
recent_trades.reasoning: Alphabet continues to exhibit tremendous relative strength, hovering near its 30-day highs amid broad AI optimism. The positive sentiment around its AI monetization makes it a prime candidate for an increased position size.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4% position in Apple to capture its strong post-earnings breakout momentum that is lifting the broader indices.
recent_trades.reasoning: Apple is breaking out to new 30-day highs following an earnings beat that has sparked a resurgence in tech bull sentiment. Allocating newly freed capital here allows us to ride the momentum as the stock helps push major averages to record highs.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Chevron entirely as Q1 earnings falter amid Middle East conflict headwinds and the ongoing closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
recent_trades.reasoning: Recent news indicates Chevron's first-quarter earnings have fallen, pressured by the ongoing Middle East conflict and persistent geopolitical risks. I am fully exiting this 4.9% position to free up capital for stronger opportunities in the tech sector.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% AAPL position on earnings-driven gap up and positive tech sector momentum with Nasdaq hitting records.

This diversifies the portfolio into direct beneficiary of current earnings/M&A tailwinds at a modest sizing given early intraday timing.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to V from 3% to 4% on crypto card spending surge (+500%) and positive Mastercard earnings read-through.
recent_trades.reasoning: Visa capturing 90% of crypto card spending (surging 500%) is a meaningful new revenue driver. Mastercard's 18% net income growth confirms strong payments environment. Stock slightly below 30-day average at 332.57 vs 335 high, offering decent entry.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Visa as payments news stays supportive and the stock remains stable within an intact uptrend, confidence 7/10.

Technically the stock is only slightly off its 30-day high and not showing damage, so nudging the weight from 3.61% to 5.0% increases exposure to a steady compounder without overcommitting.

recent_trades.summary: Adding slightly to ABBV on strong upgrade-driven sentiment and resilient defensive price action, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV carries the best sentiment in the current holdings set, helped by the Bank of America upgrade tied to Skyrizi strength and additional favorable analyst framing. The stock is holding firm near its recent range despite a mixed healthcare tape, so a small increase from 5.54% to 6.5% fits as a lower-volatility complement to the tech exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to LLY as raised guidance and persistent strength near highs keep it among the clearest defensive growth leaders, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY has one of the strongest sentiment readings in the universe, supported by explicit 2026 revenue guidance uplift and bullish commentary, while the stock is up on the day and still close to its 30-day high. The position is already working, and moving it from 5.18% toward 7.0% is a measured add that respects cash limits while leaning into a high-quality trend.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AbbVie from 2% to 3% following a Bank of America upgrade on Skyrizi strength, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV has very positive sentiment (+0.49) with an analyst upgrade citing strong Skyrizi growth and undervaluation. The position is small at 2%, and increasing to 3% aligns with positive fundamentals without exceeding risk limits.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Mastercard from 2.97% to 2% on European regulatory headwinds and negative sentiment, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: MA has negative sentiment (-0.21) from news about Europe moving to break Visa and Mastercard's grip. The stock is slightly underwater. Reducing exposure frees cash for better opportunities while maintaining a core position.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ORCL entirely due to -0.15 negative sentiment, AI debt fatigue headlines, and Wedbush noting the recent selloff as an overreaction that still lacks near-term catalysts.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has the weakest sentiment in the current holdings; combined with flat 30D performance and no supportive AI capex mentions relative to AMD/NVDA, full exit frees capital for higher-conviction names while respecting the recent-decision rule as this is new negative data.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing ABBV to 5% on highest sentiment of +0.49, BofA upgrade on Skyrizi strength, and positive analyst coverage highlighting durable growth.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV was initiated yesterday at ~2% on strong sentiment; today's even higher +0.49 score plus fresh headlines on being a top drug stock and value opportunity justify scaling to 5% while staying well under the 20% cap. Technicals show a red bar but the fundamental news flow has not been priced in.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to QCOM after the stock soared 15% on stellar Q2 earnings, doubling the position from 4.1% to ~9% on a confirmed fundamental catalyst.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM reported a stellar Q2 earnings beat with +15% price reaction, a clear fundamental catalyst that justifies increasing exposure. Currently only 4.1% weight despite strong earnings momentum. The news headline directly confirms the earnings beat. 30D low is $176.40 and current price is near that level suggesting the 15% move is the catalyst today. This is a new catalyst since last QCOM trade. Sizing to ~9% leaves room below the 20% cap while meaningfully increasing exposure to the earnings winner.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 4% position in Visa as the stock breaks out to new 30-day highs, buoyed by surging crypto card spending and massive market share.
recent_trades.reasoning: Visa is trading exactly at its 30-day high of $335.00, flashing a strong technical breakout signal. The fundamental catalyst of surging monthly crypto card spending, where Visa captures 90% market share, provides a compelling growth narrative to support the technical strength.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Eli Lilly to a 15% weight following its Q1 dominance and 2026 revenue guidance raise, signaling continued fundamental strength.
recent_trades.reasoning: Eli Lilly continues to exhibit tremendous fundamental momentum, evidenced by a strong Q1 earnings beat and an upward revision to its 2026 revenue guidance. With analyst upgrades flowing in and the stock acting as a market leader, expanding this position to 15% captures the upside of a premier healthcare asset.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the United States Oil Fund as it languishes near its 30-day lows, preferring to reallocate to higher-conviction equity opportunities.
recent_trades.reasoning: The United States Oil Fund is currently hovering just above its 30-day low of $143.14, showing distinct relative weakness. With major energy producers noting macro headwinds despite earnings beats, holding the commodity ETF directly offers less upside than targeted equities.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Oracle to free up capital, as the stock struggles for momentum amid signs of AI debt investor fatigue.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle has been trading sideways and sentiment is turning slightly negative with reports of AI debt investor fatigue. Reallocating this 4.1% weight into assets with proven earnings momentum and stronger technical setups is a more efficient use of portfolio capital.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming GS from 4% to 2% — marginal sentiment, slight negative unrealized P&L, freeing cash for earnings-catalyst names.
recent_trades.reasoning: GS has tepid sentiment at +0.07 and is down -0.86% since initiation yesterday. No fresh catalyst. Trimming to fund higher-conviction positions while maintaining small exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming MSFT further to 3% — still digesting post-earnings AI spending concerns, neutral sentiment, freeing cash for higher-conviction names.
recent_trades.reasoning: MSFT remains under pressure from AI spending concerns post-earnings with -2.56% unrealized loss and neutral sentiment at -0.03. Trimming to 3% to fund QCOM and ABBV additions. May revisit if sentiment improves.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to QCOM after stellar Q2 earnings beat with stock soaring 15%, strong mobile and AI chip demand catalysts, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM posted a blowout Q2 with 15% post-earnings surge per headlines. Currently at 3.85% weight with -1.91% unrealized P&L, but the earnings catalyst changes the picture entirely. The pre-market move suggests significant upside. Increasing to 7% to capture the momentum while remaining disciplined.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming GS as it lags in the portfolio and has less direct catalyst support than AXP or Visa today, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Goldman is slightly negative on unrealized P/L and today's headlines are only mildly positive and somewhat indirect. With stronger conviction in payments and select healthcare names, a modest trim from 5.03% to 3.5% is an efficient source of cash for reallocation.

recent_trades.summary: Cutting EOG slightly because its news flow is less direct and less compelling than other energy names in the portfolio, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Energy headlines are broadly constructive, but the most actionable positivity is centered on XOM and COP rather than EOG, and EOG-specific news is comparatively weak and neutral. Since this is only a 2.21% position with flat performance, trimming it helps fund stronger setups without materially changing the portfolio's energy exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming META as neutral sentiment and current underperformance versus other AI-linked megacaps make it a lower-priority holding, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: META is one of the few positions in the book with neutral news and a small unrealized loss, while peers like GOOGL, AMZN, and NVDA have stronger recent support. This is not a bearish exit call, but a funding source for higher-conviction additions elsewhere, so I am reducing rather than closing the position.

recent_trades.summary: Further trimming PG to 1% on continued negative sentiment and flat performance while growth names rally.
recent_trades.reasoning: PG's -0.06 sentiment and generic consumer headlines offer no edge versus AI/tech strength; recent trim already reduced it to 3% and technicals remain range-bound. Modest further cut frees capital for ABBV/MSFT while retaining small staples exposure via COST.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 5% ABBV position on highest +0.55 sentiment, BofA durable-growth forecast into mid-2030s and strong-buy commentary.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV is absent from the portfolio despite very positive news flow and technical uptrend; this adds healthcare diversification beyond TMO/PFE while sentiment contrasts sharply with negative PG. Targeting 5% keeps it well below 20% cap with room to add on further strength.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting small TSLA position as the Dawn Project report linking self-driving software to 59 deaths creates regulatory risk heading into close.
recent_trades.reasoning: TSLA was initiated as a small 0.71% position earlier today based on momentum. However, a report to Congress linking Tesla's self-driving software to 59 deaths introduces near-term regulatory risk. The position is tiny (+0.02% unrealized), so there's no meaningful gain to protect, and clearing it simplifies the portfolio for end-of-day with no material trade cost. This is a new negative catalyst not present at purchase time.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.54%, Nasdaq +0.84%, Dow +0.22%. Technology led sectors (+1.33%) while Industrials lagged (-0.76%). DeepSeek Reasoner led the day at +0.98%. 5 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek Reasoner at +19.22%. Key headline: Live Updates: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth set to face fresh round of questioning - CBS News — headwind for risk assets.
